landscaping.label: Landscaping
scopeItems: Complete a pre-start walkthrough to confirm boundaries, access, measurements, and visible site conditions.
Protect nearby surfaces, planting areas, paths, and lawn sections that are not part of the agreed work.
Prepare beds, borders, lawn edges, planting zones, and visible work areas according to the project details.
Supply labor, standard tools, and agreed materials needed to complete the listed landscaping work.
Install, shape, tidy, and finish the agreed landscaping elements with attention to drainage, levels, and presentation.
Complete a final tidy, remove routine green waste, and walk through the finished work with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be requested to reserve the schedule and cover upfront plant or material costs.
Progress payments may apply for multi-day work or material-heavy projects.
Remaining balance is due on practical completion unless different terms are agreed in writing.
assumptions: Client will provide safe access to outdoor work areas during agreed working hours.
The proposal assumes normal soil conditions and no concealed waste, rubble, services, or drainage defects.
Major grading, retaining work, tree surgery, stump grinding, or root removal are excluded unless listed in the project details.
Plant availability may require like-for-like substitutions of similar quality, size, and style.
Ongoing watering, plant establishment, and seasonal maintenance remain the client's responsibility unless added as a service.

paving.label: Paving
scopeItems: Confirm layout, dimensions, access route, drainage direction, and finish selection before work begins.
Mark out the work area and protect nearby thresholds, walls, lawns, beds, and existing surfaces where practical.
Prepare the area, including excavation, disposal, sub-base preparation, and compaction described in the project details.
Install base layers, edge restraints, bedding, and paving finish using suitable trade methods for the selected surface.
Cut, align, joint, compact, and finish paving so the completed surface is neat, stable, and ready for normal use.
Remove routine installation debris and complete a final handover check with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be required before ordering materials or reserving installation dates.
A progress payment may be requested once excavation/base preparation is complete on larger projects.
Final payment is due once installation is complete and the client has received the finished proposal scope.
assumptions: Quote assumes normal access for materials, tools, and waste removal.
The existing ground is assumed suitable for standard preparation unless otherwise stated in the project details.
Drainage changes, retaining work, major excavation, utility relocation, or remedial structural work are excluded unless stated.
Color variation, texture variation, and minor shade differences in natural or manufactured paving products are expected.
Client approvals for pattern, border, and finish selection are required before installation begins.

cleaning.label: Cleaning
scopeItems: Confirm property access, priority rooms, surface sensitivities, and any areas that should not be touched before starting.
Prepare the work area by identifying fragile items, access constraints, and any visible pre-existing damage.
Complete the cleaning tasks described in the project details using suitable products, equipment, and safe working methods.
Dust, wipe, vacuum, mop, sanitize, and detail agreed areas according to the selected service level.
Check completed rooms or work zones for missed items, streaking, residue, or obvious touch-up needs before handover.
Report access issues, damage noticed on arrival, or out-of-scope requests promptly.
paymentTerms: Payment is due on completion unless a recurring service agreement is in place.
Recurring services may be invoiced weekly, biweekly, or monthly by written agreement.
Cancellations, lockouts, or access issues inside the agreed notice period may be billable.
assumptions: Client will provide safe access, running water, electricity, and any required entry instructions.
Hazardous waste, pest activity, biohazards, and specialist restoration are excluded unless agreed separately.
Heavy furniture moving, appliance disassembly, and high-level exterior work are excluded unless stated in the project details.
Cleaning results depend on surface age, staining, mineral deposits, wear, and prior product buildup.
Client will secure valuables, confidential documents, pets, and fragile items before the appointment.

general-contractor.label: General Contractor
scopeItems: Confirm the final work area, client requirements, site access, material selections, and schedule before work begins.
Coordinate labor, standard tools, agreed materials, and any planned trade handoffs needed for the listed work.
Protect adjacent finishes and occupied areas where practical before active work begins.
Complete the project tasks described in the job details using appropriate trade practices and manufacturer guidance where applicable.
Maintain a reasonably tidy and safe work area throughout the project, including routine debris management.
Walk through completed work with the client and identify any agreed punch-list items before final handover.
paymentTerms: A deposit or staged payment may be required for scheduling, labor planning, and materials.
Change orders, added materials, and out-of-scope work must be approved before they are started.
Final balance is due when the agreed scope is complete unless a written payment schedule says otherwise.
assumptions: Permits, drawings, engineering, and inspections are excluded unless specifically included in the project details.
Hidden damage, structural defects, mold, asbestos, or unsafe conditions may require a change order.
Client decisions, material availability, inspection windows, and third-party delays may affect the final timeline.
The proposal assumes normal working access, clear work areas, and utilities available during agreed working hours.
Finish matching depends on available materials, existing conditions, and manufacturer tolerances.
